seo.title: Building Research Agents with LlamaIndex + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with LlamaIndex + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: LlamaIndex empowers developers to build sophisticated Research Agents that connect LLMs to external data via indexing and retrieval, enabling multi-step web search, analysis, and synthesis. However, these agents face risks like irrelevant retrievals, hallucinations, outdated information, and bias that can undermine reliability. Vex provides essential guardrails through verification of sources, claims, recency, and bias detection to ensure trustworthy outputs.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with LlamaIndex
architectureOverview.description:
Research Agents typically start with a query router that decomposes user questions into sub-queries for web search and RAG retrieval. LlamaIndex's VectorStoreIndex and WebReader fetch and index relevant documents, while retrievers pull context using similarity search. A synthesis engine (like OpenAI or custom LLM) analyzes multi-source data to generate summaries or answers.

The pipeline often includes tools like web search APIs (Tavily/SerpAPI) integrated via LlamaIndex agents, with query engines handling tool selection and response synthesis. Post-retrieval refinement filters irrelevant chunks, and routers manage multi-step reasoning flows.

Failure points emerge at retrieval (irrelevant context), synthesis (hallucinations), indexing (staleness), and chunking (context loss), making verification critical for production reliability.

risks.name: Retrieved context irrelevant to query
risks.description: Vector search returns semantically similar but topically unrelated documents, leading to misguided synthesis.
risks.mitigation: Wrap retriever with Vex relevance scoring and source verification before synthesis.

risks.name: Synthesizer hallucinating beyond source documents
risks.description: LLM generates claims not grounded in retrieved context, fabricating details during multi-hop reasoning.
risks.mitigation: Use Vex claim accuracy checks with citation tracing back to original sources.

risks.name: Index staleness causing outdated responses
risks.description: Cached web content or static indexes deliver expired information for time-sensitive research queries.
risks.mitigation: Implement Vex recency checks and dynamic re-indexing triggers.

risks.name: Chunk boundary errors losing critical context
risks.description: Document splitting creates chunks that break key facts across boundaries, causing incomplete retrieval.
risks.mitigation: Apply Vex context continuity verification across retrieved chunk sets.

implementation.title: Basic Research Agent with WebReader
implementation.description: Set up LlamaIndex Research Agent with web search and vector retrieval for multi-source analysis.
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader, Settings
from llama_index.readers.web import SimpleWebPageReader
from llama_index.llms.openai import OpenAI

Settings.llm = OpenAI(model="gpt-4")

documents = SimpleWebPageReader(html_to_text=True).load_data([
    "https://example-research-site.com",
    "https://news-site.com/latest"
])
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents)
query_engine = index.as_query_engine(similarity_top_k=5)

response = query_engine.query("What are the latest developments in AI agents?")
print(response)

implementation.title: Multi-Step Router Agent
implementation.description: Create a router agent that decomposes queries and selects between web search and RAG tools.
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core.agent import ReActAgent
from llama_index.core.tools import QueryEngineTool, ToolMetadata
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex

# Assume index and web_search_tool exist
rag_tool = QueryEngineTool(
    query_engine=index.as_query_engine(),
    metadata=ToolMetadata(name="rag", description="RAG over indexed docs")
)

agent = ReActAgent.from_tools([rag_tool, web_search_tool], llm=Settings.llm, verbose=True)
response = agent.chat("Compare recent AI frameworks for research agents")
print(response)

implementation.title: TypeScript LlamaIndex Node Setup
implementation.description: Initialize LlamaIndex in Node.js with vector index for research queries.

implementation.title: Custom Synthesizer for Multi-Doc
implementation.description: Build a custom synthesizer that handles multiple retrieved contexts for research synthesis.
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core.postprocessor import MetadataReplacer
from llama_index.core.query_engine import CustomQueryEngine
from llama_index.core.retrievers import VectorIndexRetriever

retriever = VectorStoreIndex.as_retriever(similarity_top_k=8)
postprocessors = [MetadataReplacer(filter_keys=['file_name'])]

def custom_synthesize(nodes, query):
    return f"Synthesized research: {query} from {len(nodes)} sources"

custom_query_engine = CustomQueryEngine(
    retriever, node_postprocessors=postprocessors, synthesizer=custom_synthesize
)
response = custom_query_engine.query("Multi-source analysis")
print(response)
